AND HE FELL DOWN ON HIS FACE TO THE EARTH. Until his nose touched the earth. Appayim (his face) refers to the nostrils which protrude from the face.2Af means a nostril; hence appayim (his face) means nostrils or nose. I.E. renders and he fell down on his face to the earth as: and he fell down on his nostrils to the earth.

matches.x: And Moses heard and fell on his face. In tractate Sanhedrin (110) they said: What news did he hear? This teaches that they suspected him of adultery, as it is said They were jealous of Moses in the camp (Psalms 106:16). For each man was jealous of him with his wife. This matter is very far from reason, and we do not learn it from the words of tradition, for who can say that this suspicion was…

AND HE FELL DOWN ON HIS FACE TO THE EARTH. Until his nose touched the earth. Appayim (his face) refers to the nostrils which protrude from the face.2Af means a nostril; hence appayim (his face) means nostrils or nose. I.E. renders and he fell down on his face to the earth as: and he fell down on his nostrils to the earth.
